codeReview.summary: The delete-mode implementation is clean, well-structured, and fully covers all requirements. Ctrl-D toggling works correctly with proper guards against create-new rows. The YES-only confirmation dialog with line editing is properly implemented. The delete script uses basenames only with realpath containment validation. Security is well-handled: canonicalize checks prevent symlink-escape attacks, and the base_path identity check prevents self-deletion. All cancel paths (Esc, Ctrl-C, non-YES input) consistently clear marks and restore Normal mode. The implementation follows existing codebase patterns for dialog state management (matching rename/graduate patterns) and script emission. Test coverage is comprehensive across unit tests in all touched modules (app, dialogs, input, render, script, cd) and the spec test suite (11/11 passed).

issues.description: make_delete_ready_app() uses std::mem::forget(dir) to leak a tempdir, preventing cleanup of test directories on disk. While this is a known Rust testing pattern to keep paths valid, it leaks directories in /tmp on every test run. Consider using a static/lazy approach or storing the TempDir handle alongside the App in tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from TDD step 3 by implementing alongside tests rather than writing tests first, citing multi-module coordination (state, rendering, input, script). This is a reasonable deviation for TUI features that span 5+ modules with interdependent types. The skill's suggested change to clarify acceptable iteration patterns for multi-module features is valid.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Added the delete-mode tests together with the implementation changes, then iterated with targeted and full test runs.' The feature touched 7 files with new types (DeleteSelection, DeleteConfirmation) threaded through app→input→render→script→cd.

summary: Delete-mode implementation passes review. All 13 expected behaviors from the feature spec are correctly implemented: Ctrl-D mark toggling with create-new guard, danger-styled rendering with trash icon, YES-only confirmation dialog with full line editing, basename-only delete scripts with realpath containment validation, and consistent cancel behavior across all modes. The code follows existing dialog patterns (matching rename/graduate), has comprehensive test coverage (282 total tests passing including new delete-specific tests and 11/11 spec tests), and the security model is sound. One minor non-blocking observation about test tempdir leaks.
